items.summary: Oil prices moved higher on Wednesday morning as markets continue to watch developments in the U.S.-Iran war.

items.headline: Treasury yields are higher as investors await key inflation report
items.summary: U.S. Treasury yields moved higher on Wednesday as investors awaited February's inflation report and monitored developments on the US-Iran war front.

items.headline: Three cargo ships struck off Iran's coast, UK says, including one in Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: The incidents mark the latest in a string of reported attacks in or near to the Strait of Hormuz, where shipping traffic has virtually ground to a halt.

items.headline: Rheinmetall sees sales growth of up to 45% in 2026, says it's in 'prime position' to arm the U.S. amid war in Iran
items.summary: Arms maker Rheinmetall reported full-year sales that grew 29% year-over-year and said revenue would grow by even more this year.

items.headline: CNBC Daily Open: Oil jitters persist as U.S. targets Iranian ships near Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: Oil was slightly higher as traders expect a group of countries to tap emergency crude reserves to mitigate disruption caused by the war in the Middle East.

items.headline: Travelers are turning to AI to plan trips — but hallucinations and trust gaps remain

items.headline: Asia-Pacific markets mostly higher as investors weigh developments in the Middle East
items.summary: Asia-Pacific markets traded mostly higher on Wednesday as investors assessed the ongoing Middle East war.

items.headline: India-China reset? Relaxed rules allow Beijing to invest in India after about six years of friction

items.headline: Iran sends millions of oil barrels to China through Strait of Hormuz even as war chokes the waterway

items.headline: How the Iran war and rising energy prices are threatening semiconductor demand

items.headline: What’s next for crude-oil prices as wealthy nations consider a release of their reserves

items.headline: CNBC Daily Open: Traders on edge despite reserve hopes
items.summary: Oil closed more than 11% lower, signaling that traders are betting the war won’t last long and the Strait of Hormuz will be reopened.
